channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SpaceX’s Starship explodes again, with wreckage seen from Florida
item.description: Nearly two months after an explosion sent flaming debris raining down on the Turks and Caicos, SpaceX launched another mammoth Starship rocket on Thursday, but lost contact minutes into the test flight as the spacecraft came tumbling down and broke apart.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: February’s IT job market in the US shows mixed signals; unemployment ticks up
item.description: “Sluggish” might be the best word to describe the IT job market after the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published its data for February employment today. The data offered mixed signals for US tech jobs: even though the number of tech roles grew, unemployment rose.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JFrog unveils JFrog ML for MLOps
item.description: JFrog has released JFrog ML, an MLOps solution designed to bring devops best practices to building, deploying, managing, and monitoring AI/ML workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spotify is leaning hard into video podcasts. Here’s how creators are adapting
item.description: Spotify just opened up a new stream of revenue for podcasters. That is, if they’re uploading video. What was once an audio-first medium, podcasting is now increasingly filmed and produced. That started on YouTube, which is now racking up one billion podcast viewers a month.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Google’s ‘quiet’ decision to remove cultural events from its Calendar app sent a loud message
item.description: Starting today, thousands of changemakers and leaders will descend upon Austin for one of the biggest festivals and conferences of the year: South by Southwest (SXSW).
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Risi, Fast Company

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Out of the lab and into the streets, researchers and doctors rally for science against Trump cuts
item.description: Researchers, doctors, their patients and supporters are venturing out of labs, hospitals and offices across the country to stand up to what they call an attack on life-saving science by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: iPhone 17 Air leaks suggest it'll get next-gen battery – and offset the 17 Pro Max's weight gains
item.description: We've got some more details on the dimensions of the iPhone 17 Air, and how it might maintain decent battery life.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Empowering women in tech: Lessons from a DWP Digital leader
item.description: I’m Head of User Support Services within DWP Digital. In simple terms, if your IT isn’t working, my team is your first protocol to fix it. I have about 450 colleagues in my function, and we support over 90,000 colleagues. I’m also Head of our IT service management profession.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: HPE Set to Axe Up to 3,000 Workers Amid Disappointing Economic Performance
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) reveals plans to lay off an estimated 2,500 workers due to disappointing economic performance, with another 500 coming in the form of attrition. The company's revenue is expected to dip by $0.7 billion in the next quarter.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: RIP (por fin) a la moda del blockchain
item.description: El bombo y platillo del blockchain que se produjo a finales de la década de 2010 casi ha desaparecido, sustituido por un intenso interés en la inteligencia artificial (IA) y perjudicado por los esquemas incompletos de criptomonedas y NFT, dicen algunos expertos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: When to choose a bare-metal cloud
item.description: I did something last week that I had not done for a while. I recommended a bare-metal cloud service to a client. They had a particular purpose, and it was the right technology to solve the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, expects Juniper buy to close year-end ’25, faces tariff issues
item.description: Despite a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its fiscal 2025, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starlink could soon tap into $42 billion federal program
item.description: The Commerce Department has changed its rules in a way that could open the door for Starlink, Elon Musk’s satellite internet service, to become part of a federal $42 billion grant program to bring high-speed service to rural and poor areas of the United States.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Without being there, Apple hits Mobile World Congress
item.description: If you overlook the introduction of the iPhone 16e with its own Apple-designed 5G modem along with the release of new iPads, Macs and processors, you might argue that Apple didn’t make an impression at this year’s Mobile World Congress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data center vacancies hit historic lows despite record construction
item.description: Space supply in major data center markets increased by 34% year-over-year to 6,922.6 megawatts, considerable growth over the 26% increase in 2023 , according to CBRE research.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: MWC25: KSA accelerates digital transformation with Huawei-Zain partnership
item.description: In a landmark move for Saudi Arabia’s digital economy, Huawei Cloud and Zain KSA have announced a strategic partnership to accelerate cloud adoption and digital transformation across industries.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google experiments with AI-only search as competition heats up
item.description: Google has launched an experimental search mode that replaces traditional links with AI-generated summaries, as it ramps up efforts to integrate AI amid growing competition. Currently, Google Search displays AI Overviews, which provide summaries above traditional search results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The key new features in .NET 10
item.description: The first preview of the next long-term support release of .NET is now available. Like previous even-numbered releases, .NET 10 builds on features introduced in the current short-term release, with a focus on performance and reliability improvements. As .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to handle type erasure in advanced Java generics
item.description: Generics programming in Java enhances type safety and code reusability by allowing developers to define classes and methods using type parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Utah just passed the country’s first age-verification bill for app stores. But the fight isn’t over
item.description: On Wednesday, Utah became the first state in the country to pass legislation mandating that app stores verify users’ ages and get parental consent for certain activity on minors’ accounts.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI driving a 165% rise in data center power demand by 2030
item.description: Global power demand from data centers will increase 50% by 2027 and by as much as 165% by the end of the decade compared with 2023, according to a recent report from Goldman Sachs Research The industry is shrugging off the potential of DeepSeek – which claimed large-scale AI modeling capabilities fr
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The best Java and JVM language frameworks
item.description: Not long ago in this space, we compared the 10 best front-end JavaScript frameworks. Now we turn our piercing gaze to the ample supply of back-end frameworks, starting with frameworks built for Java and JVM languages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to boost Python program performance with Zig
item.description: Python might not be the fastest of languages, but it has many advantages. Users enjoy Python’s convenience and development speed, as well as its broad ecosystem of libraries. Something else people appreciate about Python is how it bridges language ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top data storage certifications to sharpen your skills
item.description: Enterprise data storage skills are in demand, and that means storage certifications can be more valuable to organizations looking for people with those qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: JDK 25 kicks off with a stable values API
item.description: While Java Development Kit 25 is not set to arrive until September, the first feature already has been proposed for it — a preview of a stable values API that promises to improve startup of Java applications. Stable values are objects that hold immutable data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PIX 11
